message:
Propagate errors from JOIN ON/WHERE predicates instead of silently swallowing them

Previously, eval_join_predicate() errors in both the WHERE filter
(step 5 of query execution) and the JOIN ON constraint evaluation
were silently swallowed via .unwrap_or(false), meaning a typo in a
column name like "t1.missing" would silently produce empty results
instead of reporting an error.

Changes:
- connection.rs: replaced .retain() with .unwrap_or(false) in WHERE
  filter with an explicit loop that propagates errors via the ?
  operator; removed now-unnecessary #[allow(clippy::unnecessary_wraps)]
  on execute_single_join; changed JOIN ON evaluation to use ? instead
  of unwrap_or(false)
- Added two regression tests: test_join_on_unknown_column_errors and
  test_join_where_unknown_column_errors to verify that unknown columns
  in JOIN contexts now correctly return "column not found" errors
- codegen.rs: related VDBE codegen improvements
- lib.rs: related top-level integration changes

This brings JOIN error semantics closer to SQLite's behavior where
referencing non-existent columns is always an error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
